messages.content: What are those things on the shoulders of old fashion military uniforms called?
Those things on the shoulders of old fashion military uniforms are called epaulettes.

Translate to English

“…qui vicerit possidebit haec et ero illi Deus et ille erit mihi filius”
"…he who conquers will possess these things, and I will be his God and he will be my son."
What is the origin of this quote?
This quote is from the Bible, specifically from the book of Revelation, chapter 21, verse 7.
What is Easter Monday?

Easter Monday is the day after Easter Sunday, which is a Christian holiday that celebrates the resurrection of Jesus Christ. It is observed in many countries as a public holiday, and is a day for people to spend time with family, attend church services, and participate in various Easter-related activities. In some countries, Easter Monday is also marked by special events and celebrations, such as parades, games, and egg hunts.
Okay, but why is it on a Monday and who celebrates it?

The celebration of Easter Monday as a holiday is primarily observed in Christian countries that follow the Gregorian calendar, such as the United States, Canada, Australia, and many European nations. 

In the Western Christian tradition, Easter Monday marks the second day of the octave of Easter, which is the period of eight days following Easter Sunday. Easter Monday is considered to be a continuation of the joyous celebration of Easter Sunday, and is often celebrated with feasting, parades, and other festivities.

In many Eastern Orthodox and Eastern Catholic churches, Easter Monday is not observed as a public holiday. Instead, the day following Easter Sunday is known as "Bright Monday," and is celebrated with religious services and other observances that emphasize the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
What is a bugbear?

A bugbear is a common  term to describe a cause of distress, anxiety or irritation. It can also refer to a mythical creature that is often depicted as a variant of the hobgoblin, usually as a hairy, bear-like creature. In this context, it is sometimes used as a synonym for a bogeyman, which is a mythical creature in many cultures used to frighten children into good behavior.
What wavelength range do military-grade infrared goggles see in?

Military-grade infrared goggles generally operate in the short-wave infrared (SWIR) or mid-wave infrared (MWIR) spectrum, typically between 0.7 microns to 5 microns wavelength. This allows them to detect and enhance the infrared radiation emitted by objects and people, which is particularly useful for conducting covert operations in low-light or nighttime conditions. Some advanced models can also operate in the long-wave infrared (LWIR) spectrum, which covers a range of 8 to 14 microns and enables the detection of even fainter thermal signatures.
Is daylight to bright for cats?

Cats are able to see in daylight without being affected by the brightness, as their eyes have a mechanism to adjust to different levels of light. Cats have a unique structure in their eyes called the tapetum lucidum, which enables them to see better in low light conditions compared to humans. This structure reflects light back onto the retina, increasing their sensitivity to light and improving their vision in dimly lit environments. However, extremely bright light can temporarily overwhelm their eyes and cause discomfort or temporary vision impairment, just as it can for humans. It is important to note that prolonged exposure to direct sunlight can also be harmful to cats, causing eye damage or even blindness.
What oxidizer burns a greenish-yellow?

The oxidizer that burns a greenish-yellow color is typically identified as Barium Nitrate (Ba(NO3)2). This is often used in pyrotechnics to create a bright green light that is visible from a distance, and it can also be used in other applications, such as in flares and signal devices.

Translate to Latin

"In this sign, conquer!"
In Latin, "In this sign, conquer!" translates to "In hoc signo vinces!"
What would you call a chemical compound that is made up of one carbon atom and two fluorine atoms.
The chemical compound made up of one carbon atom and two fluorine atoms is called Carbon Difluoride, with the chemical formula CF2. It is also referred to as difluorocarbon or carbon fluoride.
What happens when liquid fluoride is exposed to STP?

When liquid fluoride is exposed to STP (Standard Temperature and Pressure conditions), its behavior and properties can vary depending on the specific type of fluoride compound. 

For instance, some liquid fluorides, such as liquid sodium fluoride (NaF) or potassium fluoride (KF), have high boiling points and remain in a liquid state at room temperature under standard atmospheric pressure. They are highly reactive and corrosive to many materials, including many metals and organic compounds.

On the other hand, other liquid fluorides such as sulfur hexafluoride (SF6) and carbon tetrafluoride (CF4) have very low boiling points and will quickly vaporize under STP conditions, forming a gas that is heavier than air. These compounds are used in a variety of industrial and electrical applications as insulators, solvents, and other specialized functions.
What happens if Sodium fluoride (NaF) mixes with Potassium fluoride (KF)?

If sodium fluoride (NaF) mixes with potassium fluoride (KF), a new compound called sodium potassium fluoride (NaKF) is formed. Sodium potassium fluoride is a compound with the chemical formula NaKF, which contains equal amounts of sodium cations and potassium anions. NaF and KF are both salts that readily dissolve in water, and when mixed together, they will quickly ionize and react to form the new compound. 

Sodium potassium fluoride has a variety of applications in industry, including as a flux for aluminum welding, a catalyst in polymerization reactions, and an additive in dental products to prevent tooth decay. The physical properties of this compound are dependent on the proportions of NaF and KF in the mixture, which can be tailored to meet the specific needs of different applications.
What was the last element discovered?

The last element to be officially recognized and the most recently discovered is Tennessine (Ts). Tennessine was first synthesized in 2010 by a joint team of Russian and American scientists at the Joint Institute for Nuclear Research (JINR) in Russia and the Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL) in the United States. Its existence was confirmed by the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) in late 2016, and it was named after the state of Tennessee, where the ORNL is located. Tennessine is a synthetic element with the atomic number 117, and it is highly unstable, decaying within milliseconds of its creation.
What is a valence electron? Explain in the simplest terms possible.

Valence electrons are the electrons that occupy the outermost energy level of an atom. They determine how an atom bonds with other atoms and how it reacts in chemical reactions. These electrons are responsible for the chemical properties of an element, as they are involved in the formation of chemical bonds. The valence electrons are found in the electron cloud or shell surrounding the nucleus of the atom and are typically represented by dots or circles around the atomic symbol. The number of valence electrons an element has determines its position on the periodic table and its chemical behavior.

Does the following count as a three-piece suit?
- A pair of jeans
- A Pink Floyd "Dark Side of The Moon" t-shirt
- A green hoodie

No, the outfit described does not count as a three-piece suit. A three-piece suit is typically composed of a matching jacket, trousers, and a waistcoat (vest). The outfit in question seems to be a casual outfit consisting of a pair of jeans, a t-shirt, and a hoodie.
How does Selenium-74 behave at room temperature?

Selenium-74 is a radioactive isotope of selenium with a half-life of about 110 days. At room temperature, it decays by beta emission, which means it releases high-energy electrons as it transforms into other elements. This decay process produces gamma radiation, which gives off high-frequency electromagnetic waves that can penetrate matter.

Because of its radioactivity, Selenium-74 is generally handled with care in specialized facilities, such as nuclear power plants and research laboratories. The decay of Selenium-74 can be monitored using radiation detectors, which can measure the level of radiation emitted and allow for its safe handling.

In terms of its chemical behavior, Selenium-74 behaves similarly to other isotopes of selenium, forming stable compounds with other elements, such as hydrogen, oxygen, and carbon. These compounds can have a variety of industrial and biological applications, including as nutritional supplements, dyes, and semiconductor materials in electronics.
Selenium-74 is stable and doesn't decay

I apologize for my mistake. You are correct - Selenium-74 is actually considered a stable isotope of selenium and does not undergo radioactive decay under normal conditions. Unlike radioactive isotopes such as Selenium-75 or Selenium-79, which have unstable nuclei and emit radiation as they decay, Selenium-74 is a non-radioactive isotope that does not emit radiation and is not considered hazardous. 

At room temperature, Selenium-74 behaves similarly to other stable isotopes of selenium in terms of its chemical properties, forming compounds with a variety of other elements and used in a range of industrial and biological applications. Once again, I apologize for any confusion my previous response may have caused.
How does Selenium behave at room temperature?

Selenium is a chemical element that is a non-metal and belongs to the group of chalcogens. It exists in several allotropic forms, with gray crystalline selenium and amorphous red selenium being the most common forms at room temperature. 

At room temperature, selenium is a solid that is brittle and has a metallic gray appearance. It is insoluble in water but dissolves in acids and some other nonpolar organic solvents.  Selenium can be highly toxic to humans and animals if ingested in large amounts, but it is also an essential nutrient required in small quantities for proper physiological function.

Selenium has many important applications, including as a component in certain alloys, as a photovoltaic material in solar cells, as a toner in photocopiers, and as a dietary supplement. It is also widely used in the electronics, glass, and pharmaceutical industries, and is a component of some animal feed, fertilizers, and fungicides.
I found a jar filled with a red powered substance. It's apparently Selenium. Why is it like this?

Selenium can exist in different allotropic forms, such as gray crystalline selenium and amorphous red selenium, depending on the conditions under which it is produced. The red powder you found is likely to be the amorphous form of selenium, which is a non-crystalline form of the element that does not have a defined crystal structure. 

Amorphous selenium is typically produced by melting and rapidly cooling the element, resulting in a powder that has a bright red color. This powder is insoluble in water and many organic solvents, and can be used in a range of industrial applications, such as in the production of photoconductive coatings, photovoltaic cells, and metallurgical alloys. 

It is important to note that selenium can be highly toxic in certain forms and concentrations, so it is advised to handle the substance with care and to keep it properly stored to avoid any accidental ingestion or inhalation. If you are unsure about the source or handling of the substance you found, it is recommended to seek advice from a qualified chemist or toxicologist.
